Class Transaction

java.lang.Object
Transaction.Transaction
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Transaction extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a financial transaction (income or expense).

Stores details such as amount, category, description, and date. Implements Serializable for data persistence.

See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Defines the possible types of a transaction.

  • Constructor Details

    • Transaction

      public Transaction(int userID, double amount, String category, String description, String notes, Transaction.TransactionType typeEnum, LocalDateTime dateTime)
      Constructs a new Transaction with a specific user ID.
      Parameters:
      userID - the ID of the user who owns this transaction
      amount - the transaction amount
      category - the category name
      description - a short description
      notes - additional notes
      typeEnum - the type (INCOME or EXPENSE)
      dateTime - the date and time of the transaction

    • Transaction

      public Transaction(double amount, String category, String description, String notes, Transaction.TransactionType typeEnum, LocalDateTime dateTime)
      Constructs a new Transaction without a specific user ID (defaults to 0).
      Parameters:
      amount - the transaction amount
      category - the category name
      description - a short description
      notes - additional notes
      typeEnum - the type (INCOME or EXPENSE)
      dateTime - the date and time of the transaction

  • Method Details

    • save

      public boolean save()
      Validates this transaction for saving.
      Returns:
      true if the amount is valid and category is provided; false otherwise

    • delete

      public void delete()
      Soft deletes this transaction by setting its amount to zero.

    • edit

      public void edit(String category, String description)
      Edits the category and description of this transaction.
      Parameters:
      category - the new category (ignored if blank)
      description - the new description (ignored if blank)

    • updateSpentAmount

      public void updateSpentAmount(double newAmount)
      Updates the transaction amount.
      Parameters:
      newAmount - the new transaction amount

    • getRecentTransactions

      public static List<Transaction> getRecentTransactions(int userID, int count)
      Retrieves a list of recent transactions for a user.
      Parameters:
      userID - the ID of the user
      count - the maximum number of transactions to retrieve
      Returns:
      a list of recent transactions (currently returns empty list)

    • getType

      public String getType()
      Returns a short string representing the transaction type.
      Returns:
      "IN" for income, "EX" for expense

    • getDate

      public LocalDateTime getDate()
      Returns the date and time of this transaction.
      Returns:
      the transaction date

    • toSummaryString

      public String toSummaryString()
      Returns a summarized string representation of this transaction.
      Returns:
      a summary string containing ID, type, category, amount, and description

    • getTransactionID

      public int getTransactionID()
      Returns:
      the unique transaction ID

    • getUserID

      public int getUserID()
      Returns:
      the associated user ID

    • getAmount

      public double getAmount()
      Returns:
      the transaction amount

    • getCategory

      public String getCategory()
      Returns:
      the transaction category

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Returns:
      the transaction description

    • getNotes

      public String getNotes()
      Returns:
      additional notes

    • getTypeEnum

      public Transaction.TransactionType getTypeEnum()
      Returns:
      the transaction type enum

    • getDateTime

      public LocalDateTime getDateTime()
      Returns:
      the full date and time

    • getFormattedDate

      public String getFormattedDate()
      Formats the transaction date into a readable string.
      Returns:
      the formatted date string (dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm)

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a fully detailed string representation of this transaction.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string containing all major transaction details
